question_text: The Accredited Representative of the Federal Republic of Nigeria is:
option_b: Diplomatic or Consular Representative of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.
option_c: Accountant General of the Federation.
option_d: Special Adviser to the President on Foreign Affairs.

question_text: Civil Service of the Federation is a
option_a: Parastatals of the Government of the Federation.
option_b: All staff of the Nigerian National Petroleum Corporation (NNPC) Plc.
option_c: Body or Organ which enjoys continuity of existence.
option_d: All the Personal Assistants to the Accounting Officers of the Ministries, Departments and Agencies (MDAs).

question_text: Classified Correspondence is a correspondence which has been graded:
option_b: Formal and informal
option_c: Interactive and engaging
option_d: Restricted, Confidential, Secret or Top Secret.

question_text: The Appointment of junior staff on grade level 06 and below is handled by:
option_a: Head of the Civil Service of the Federation only.
option_b: Accountant General of the Federation in conjunction with the IPPIS Office.
option_c: Junior Staff Committee as approved by the Accounting Officer of a Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Office.
option_d: Secretary to the Government of the Federation.

question_text: An appointment of officers of grade level 07-17 is handled by
option_b: Accountant General of the Federation in conjunction with the IPPIS Office.
option_c: Special Adviser to the President of the Federal Republic of Nigeria on labour and productivity.
option_d: Secretary to the Government of the Federation.

question_text: The submission of applications for the appointment of officers of grade level 07-17 is within:
option_b: Three months
option_c: Six weeks
option_d: Twenty-one working days

question_text: An annual competitive Public/Civil Service Entry Examination for is held for new entrants and tranferring serving officers on Grade Level:
option_b: Grade Level 15-17
option_c: Grade Level 12-14
option_d: Grade Level 07-10

question_text: Candidates with required qualifications who are to be appointed into the State/ Zonal Offices of a Ministry/ Extra-Ministerial Office must be:
option_a: Candidates who studied in any Higher Institution in those States/ Zones
option_b: Residents of those States/ Zones
option_c: Candidates who did their NYSC Service in those States/ Zones.
option_d: Residents of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT).

question_text: The incremental date of an Officer appointed, or promoted to a post in the Federal Public Service is...............on the condition that such Officer has spent at least...........on the Grade with good performance before enjoying the increment.
option_a: 31st December of every year, twelve (12) months
option_b: 1st June of every year, five (5) months
option_c: 31st March of every year, three (3) months
option_d: 1st January of every year, six (6) months

question_text: Seniority in the Public Service is based on the.................. certified by an authorized Officer and or..................as reflected in the appropriate register and career progression of the concerned Officers.
option_a: Exit date from the previous employment, date of last promotion examination
option_b: Approved leave date from the initial engagement, date of last entry examination
option_c: Date on the last pay certificate, date of previous employment pay slip.
option_d: Entry date/the date of assumption of duty, date of present appointment.

question_text: The employment of unpaid staff into the public service is..........
option_b: allowed
option_c: discretional
option_d: Circumstantial.

question_text: A Staff Record Form is also called.............., a copy of which must be sent to the National Record Centre in the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation, Accountant General's Office, Auditor General's Office and PenCom within............. of the appointment
option_b: Letter of Administration (LoA) Seven (7) days
option_c: Last Pay Certiifcate (LPC) Twelve (12) Months
option_d: Staff Pay Slip (SPL) Six (6) weeks.

question_text: Any change of date of birth in the record of service or other personnel records upon employment into the Public Service is regarded as..............
option_b: A mere mistake.
option_c: An act of serious misconduct.
option_d: An act of proper use of affidavit of change of birth date.

question_text: ...............means the filling of vacancies by direct appointment of persons not already in any of the Public Service of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.
option_b: Promotion
option_c: Recruitment
option_d: Condonation

question_text: A person with requisite qualification can be employed into the public service as a trainee or pupil, on probation in a pensionable post, on non-pensionable contract, acting appointment and.................
option_b: Appointment based on recommendation from a High Chief or Political Head.
option_c: Casual appointment
option_d: Special appointment.

question_text: The Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC)'s employment of.......................is automatic irrespective of vacancy constraints:
option_a: Confidential Driver to the President of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.
option_b: Orderly to the Honourable Minister of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT).
option_c: National Youth Service Corps. (NYSC) Presidential Merit Award Winners.
option_d: Chief Chef to the First Lady of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.

question_text: .....................of all recruitments in the Service are to be reserved for.....................
option_a: 12% Confidential Driver to the President of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.
option_b: 20% Orderly to the Honourable Minister of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT).
option_c: 10% Chief Chef to the First Lady of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.
option_d: 5% persons with disability.

question_text: Eligibility for appointing every applicant into the Federal Public Service are (i) Between the age of 18 and 50 years; (ii) Having minimum qualification
option_a: s contained in the scheme of service; (iii)Having being certified by an authorized Healthcare Provider as medically fit; (iv) Having a testimonial of good conduct from the last employer or school attended; (v) Having requisite qualification as contained in the scheme of service; and
option_b: any officer seeking for an appointment into the Federal Public Service must seek clearance from the National Pension Commission.
option_c: no officer should seek Federal Government's appointment without Police extract.
option_d: every officer must possess a bank guarantee to confirm his/her loan and debt status.
review_notes: Option A label was malformed in source: "as contained in the scheme of service; (iii)Having being certified by an authorized Healthcare Provider as medically fit; (iv) Having a testimonial of good conduct from the last employer or school attended;".

Duplicate option label A found in source: "a. no officer shall be appointed into the Federal Public Service without authorized establishment and recruitment waiver from the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.".

question_text: No candidate would be considered for an appointment into the Federal Public Service for the following reasons except:
option_a: he has been convicted of a criminal offence.
option_b: he has been dismissed or called upon to resign or retire from the Public Service before.
option_c: he has left his previous employment without reasons and whether he is free from financial embarrassment.
option_d: he is into agricultural value chain.

question_text: When a newly appointed Officer assumes duty, he must enter his personal particulars on..........
option_b: Form Gen. 60
option_c: Staff salary form
option_d: Form TFL 10
